editor_settings.title: Is the Canon PowerShot G7 X Mark II Still Good?
editor_settings.content:
This camera refuses to die. Years after release, it keeps popping up in vlogs, resale listings, and TikTok clips. That usually means one of two things: it’s either genuinely solid, or massively overhyped.

In this case, it’s a bit of both.

Quick Verdict

The Canon G7X Mark II is still a very good compact camera for casual creators and everyday shooting. It delivers reliable image quality and great colors in a pocket-sized body. But at today’s inflated resale prices, it’s not always the smartest buy.

[product-card:canon-powershot-g7x-mark-ii-1010]

Who It’s For / Who Should Skip

Buy it if:

  • You want a simple, high-quality point-and-shoot for travel or lifestyle content
  • You care about Canon’s color science more than technical specs
  • You don’t want to deal with lenses or complicated setups

Skip it if:

  • You shoot a lot of video and need 4K
  • You want fast autofocus for action or sports
  • You can stretch your budget to newer compact cameras

Design & First Impressions

Pick it up and you immediately understand the appeal. It’s compact, slightly grippy, and doesn’t feel cheap. The tilting touchscreen is responsive and makes shooting from awkward angles easy.

The control ring around the lens adds a bit of manual feel without overwhelming beginners. It’s a camera that doesn’t get in your way, which is exactly why people keep using it.

@witcamera กล้อง canon G7x mark ii ถ่ายวิววิวมากกกก🌆 #g7xmarkii #ถ่ายไงก็สวย #ถ่ายไงได้งี้ #กล้องดิจิตอล #รีวิวกล้อง #camera #sunset #ถ่ายรูป #fyp ♬ เสียงต้นฉบับ - กล้องมือสอง

Specs That Actually Matter

  • 20.1MP 1-inch sensor
    Big enough to outperform phones, especially in low light
  • f/1.8–2.8 lens
    This is the real star. It gives you background blur and better night shots
  • No 4K video
    This is the biggest limitation today
  • Compact size
    Fits in your pocket, which means you actually carry it

Specs aren’t impressive on paper in 2026. But in real use, they still hold up better than expected.

Real-World Performance

Photos are where this camera shines. Colors look natural without heavy editing. Skin tones are especially strong, which is why vloggers loved it in the first place.

Low light performance is decent for its size. Not amazing, but clearly better than most smartphones in similar conditions.

Autofocus is fine for casual use. It struggles with fast-moving subjects and can feel dated compared to newer cameras.

Video is where it shows its age. No 4K, and stabilization is just okay. It works for simple clips, but not for serious content creation anymore.

For Sale!

Canon PowerShot G7X Mark II – 20.1MP Digital Camera

This camera is in pristine, like-new condition, having been used only twice. It has a screen protector installed and includes everything in the original box.

What’s Included:
✔️ Canon PowerShot G7X Mark II
$500 pic.twitter.com/wEN5J6Nl15

— JAMES PRESCOTT 🏌️‍♂️ 🏌️‍♂️ (@scottyfors3874) May 7, 2025

Real-World Performance

Photos are where this camera shines. Colors look natural without heavy editing. Skin tones are especially strong, which is why vloggers loved it in the first place.

Low light performance is decent for its size. Not amazing, but clearly better than most smartphones in similar conditions.

Autofocus is fine for casual use. It struggles with fast-moving subjects and can feel dated compared to newer cameras.

Video is where it shows its age. No 4K, and stabilization is just okay. It works for simple clips, but not for serious content creation anymore.

The Hype Factor

The G7X Mark II lives on because of social media. TikTok and YouTube creators brought it back into the spotlight as a “hidden gem” camera.

The reality is simpler. It’s not hidden, and it’s not cutting-edge. It’s just reliable, easy, and flattering for everyday content. That combination keeps it relevant.

Pricing Reality Check

At launch, this camera sat comfortably in the mid-range compact category.

Today:

  • Retail or refurbished: around $600 to $750
  • Resale market: often $450 to $650 depending on condition

But if you look beyond the obvious listings, prices drop quite a bit. Some quieter marketplaces and less polished listings land closer to $350 to $420.

That’s where it starts making real sense again.

At $600, it’s hard to justify.
At $400, it becomes interesting.

Comparison: What Else Should You Consider?

Sony ZV-1

  • Better autofocus
  • 4K video
  • More modern overall
  • Worse battery life

Canon G7X Mark III

  • Adds 4K and better video features
  • More expensive
  • Slightly improved performance

Smartphones (High-End)

  • More convenient
  • Better video processing
  • Worse natural depth and lens flexibility

If video matters, skip the Mark II. If photos and simplicity matter, it still competes.

Is It Worth It?

Yes, but only at the right price.

You’re not buying cutting-edge tech. You’re buying a proven, easy-to-use camera that still delivers good results with minimal effort.

The problem is not the camera. It’s the inflated perception around it.

Final Verdict

The Canon PowerShot G7 X Mark II is still good. Not amazing, not outdated, just solid.

Buy it if you find it at a reasonable price and want a simple, reliable shooter. Skip it if you expect modern features or plan to do serious video work.

It’s not magic. It’s just dependable. And sometimes, that’s enough
